commands.review_summary:
The PR reformats the README intro by splitting the supported-channel sentence and quick-link row into Markdown lists without changing the stated links or channel names.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a documentation formatting PR rather than a bug report. The relevant check is diff review against the current README intro lines, not a runtime reproduction.
commands.review_followup: Manual review is appropriate because the remaining blocker is contributor-provided visible proof and maintainer editorial acceptance, not an automated repair.

target.title: docs: improve README link formatting
target.body:
## Summary
- Split the dense supported-channel sentence into a short Markdown list.
- Split the long quick-link row into grouped Markdown list entries.
- Keep the same README content and links while making the rendered intro easier to scan.

## Real behavior proof
- Fetched the upstream README and applied this README-only formatting change locally.
- Verified the fork branch README matches the local README content.
- Ran a local Markdown sanity check confirming fenced code blocks are balanced.
- Ran a local link check for README relative links after fetching LICENSE, VISION.md, and CONTRIBUTING.md.
- Checked there is no trailing whitespace.

## Not tested
- Did not run pnpm build/check/test because this is a README-only formatting change with no runtime code changes.

AI-assisted: yes. I reviewed the final diff and understand the change.
target.author: ankitvd6
